title: Trade and International Business: Research & Analysis | CSIS
text:
Trade and International Business

Reciprocal tariffs, threats to global supply chains, and comparative manufacturing advantages have disrupted the established global trade system. CSIS scholars provide analysis and commentary on the international trading system and its institutions.

Image

Photo: 3dmitry/Adobe Stock

Keeping Aluminum in Allied Hands: The Stakes of Alcoa’s South Africa Bet

Alcoa has made the largest U.S. mining supply chain investment in South Africa in years. Whether it marks the beginning of a new wave of Western investment will depend on South Africa’s policy choices—and its ability to compete for strategic mining capital.

Commentary
by
Gracelin Baskaran

— July 8, 2026

Was This World Cup More Political? | State of Play

Please join CSIS experts to assess the geopolitical dimensions of this year's World Cup in a live episode of State of Play. 

Event

— July 16, 2026

China and the Section 301 Investigations: The Legal, Economic, and Diplomatic Issues

Scott Kennedy hosts a virtual event with expert panelists to discuss the ongoing Section 301 investigations into how forced labor and overcapacity could be harming U.S. trade interests.

Event

— July 13, 2026

The Next Phase of USMCA

In this week's column, Bill Reinsch argues that the USMCA review will prolong uncertainty, delaying investment and complicating North American trade negotiations.

Commentary
by
William Alan Reinsch

— July 8, 2026

Keeping Aluminum in Allied Hands: The Stakes of Alcoa’s South Africa Bet

Alcoa has made the largest U.S. mining supply chain investment in South Africa in years. Whether it marks the beginning of a new wave of Western investment will depend on South Africa’s policy choices—and its ability to compete for strategic mining capital.

Commentary
by
Gracelin Baskaran

— July 8, 2026

The Trade Guys

USMCA Review Deadline with CSIS's Diego Marroquín Bitar

Bill and Scott welcome Diego Marroquín Bitar, fellow with the CSIS Americas Program, following the July 1, 2026, USMCA review deadline to discuss the U.S. decision not to renew and the future of the USMCA.

Podcast Episode
by

Diego Marroquín Bitar,

and
William Alan Reinsch

— July 7, 2026

Image

Export Nation or Ecosystem Power: South Korea’s Choice in the AI Industrial Age

Navin Girishankar argues South Korea's second "Miracle on the Han River" hinges on becoming an ecosystem power—fusing capital, standards, tech, and know-how for the AI age.

Report
by
Navin Girishankar

— July 7, 2026

The United States Has Opted Not to Extend the USMCA

The United States declined to renew the USMCA for another 16 years, triggering a decade of annual reviews. But the deal did not collapse. It now faces a slower, costlier threat: prolonged uncertainty that stalls investment across North America.

Commentary
by
CSIS Scholars

— July 2, 2026

Troubled Straits: Analyzing Trade Chokepoints in the South China Sea

This CSIS brief offers groundbreaking insight into the value and types of goods flowing through eight different South China Sea chokepoints. The analysis reveals each country’s reliance on each chokepoint and challenges assumptions about their relative importance.

Brief
by
CSIS Scholars

— July 1, 2026

Fortress North America: Mexico’s Role in Securing the Region’s Economic Future

The 2026 USMCA review is the first test of whether the United States, Mexico, and Canada can turn economic security into a regional framework. Mexico is the hinge, as the top U.S. trading partner, but is also importing from China at a 14-to-1 ratio.

Report
by
Diego Marroquín Bitar

— July 1, 2026

Troubled Straits: Analyzing Trade Chokepoints in the South China Sea

This digital feature and dashboard offer groundbreaking data and insights into trade flows through critical chokepoints in the South China Sea.

Digital Feature
by
CSIS Scholars

— June 30, 2026
